summary: The share of families with children returning to shelter within a year of an unsubsidized exit has fallen by close to half since FY22. DHS attributes the broader decline in shelter returns for families to the city's growing investment in subsidized rental assistance, including CityFHEPS, which it says generates more sustained, permanent placements than exits without a subsidy.
oneLiner: decreasing as subsidized rental assistance produces more stable exits citywide
